mission.title: Install and Configure Kubescape on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kubescape is an open-source Kubernetes security platform that provides comprehensive security coverage, including risk analysis, compliance, and misconfiguration scanning for your Kubernetes clusters. Installing Kubescape helps ensure your Kubernetes environments are secure and compliant with best practices.

steps.title: Add the Kubescape Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Kubescape Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add kubescape https://kubescape.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kubescape using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kubescape in the 'kubescape' namespace, creating it if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install kubescape kubescape/kubescape --namespace kubescape --create-namespace --version v4.0.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Kubescape pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubescape
```

steps.title: Expose the Kubescape service
steps.description:
Port-forward the Kubescape service to access it locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/kubescape -n kubescape 8080:80
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Kubescape deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment kubescape -n kubescape --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kubescape will show the pods running in the 'kubescape' namespace. You can access the Kubescape UI by navigating to http://localhost:8080 in your web browser.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubescape https://kubescape.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install kubescape kubescape/kubescape --namespace kubescape --create-namespace --version v4.0.2

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Kubescape Helm release from the 'kubescape' namespace to remove the deployment and associated resources.
```bash
helm uninstall kubescape --namespace kubescape
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation.
```bash
kubectl delete crd kubescape --ignore-not-found
kubectl delete pvc --all -n kubescape
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'kubescape' namespace and verify that it has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kubescape
kubectl get namespaces | grep kubescape
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Kubescape deployment and any associated resources.
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubescape -o yaml > kubescape-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Kubescape release to the latest version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kubescape kubescape/kubescape --namespace kubescape --version v4.1.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Kubescape pods are running the new version successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubescape
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Kubescape pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubescape
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Kubescape service, ensure that the port-forwarding command is running and check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n kubescape
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource allocation issues, verify the resource limits and requests set for the deployment.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment kubescape -n kubescape
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If Helm reports that the release is not found, ensure you are using the correct namespace and release name.
```bash
helm list --namespace kubescape
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
